The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Crafturday

events.title: Builders Club

events.description: Join Fay & Fluffy (stars of Family Jr's The Fabulous Show with Fay & Fluffy) as they share some of their favourite books and stories. Fay & Fluffy's main goal is to share the message of inclusivity wherever they go, cheering on all the kids who see them to be proud of who they are, loving themselves and being kind to one another. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: Traditional Lion Dance
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month as the Chinatown Community Lion Dance Group performs the traditional Lion Dance with drumming, cymbals and a Chinese Lion paraded through the branch. This will be followed by a short talk to explore the cultural significance, and interactive stations for children.

events.title: Intro to Chess
events.description: Chess is for everyone! Learn the basics of the game including how to set up the board, how to move pieces, and how to win. All are welcome – no registration required

events.title: Girls Coding
events.description: This program introduces children to beginner computer programming and fosters the development of essential STEM skills. Delivered in collaboration with Macaulay Centres for Children, the initiative focuses on promoting educational growth and enhancing future employment opportunities, particularly for children in marginalized communities. Participants will engage in hands-on coding activities, make new friends, and have fun in a supportive. Space is limited, and advance registration with a commit

events.title: Henna Art Workshop for Children
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month and discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. Drop In. Space is limited. Please Note: Henna will be available on a first-come, first-served basis. The final henna application will begin no later than 2:55 pm. No new designs will be started after this time.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Chess and Board Game Café Drop In
events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome!

events.title: Movie Screening: Zootopia 2
events.description: Come join us for a family movie screening at the library! Rookie cops Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde find themselves on the twisting trail of a great mystery when Gary De'Snake arrives in Zootopia and turns the animal metropolis upside down. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before.  Rated PG. Drop-in.  Zootopia 2

events.title: Sunday STEAM Stations
events.description: Build, create, learn & play at various stations including Snap Circuits, LEGO, KEVA Planks, Connectors, & Colouring! This is a program for kids, teens, & families.  All are welcome!  Drop-in, no registration required. Please note: there is no program on Sun May 17, as TPL will be closed for observation of the Victoria Day holiday.

events.title: Sunday Family Movies
events.description: For the Month of April we'll be hosting Sunday Family Movies! Bring friends, family and a snack to wind-down while we watch movies anyone can enjoy.  Drop-in  All ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Coding with Scratch
events.description: Learn the basics of 3D design with Tinkercad - a free web app for everyone. By combining simple shapes, it is possible to create complex designs and potentially, 3D print the result with ease. Participants will create a custom keychain to be 3D printed and available for pick-up in the days following the program. Prerequisites : Mouse and keyboarding skills required. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Li

events.title: Teen Video Game Social
events.description: Drop in during lunch and play some video games! Games include Minecraft (PS4), Naruto Shippuden 4 (PS4), Need for Speed (PS4), NBA 2K25 (PS4) and more! Space and supplies limited. Please note that this program is for Teens.

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Early Modern Era

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Sir Henry Pellatt and the English Trip of 1910
events.description: Mima Brown Kapches, an archaeologist and former Curator of the Royal Ontario Museum, presents and discusses her Canadian historical non-fiction book, The English Trip of 1910 . This lively and fascinating account of a military expedition of over 600 Toronto men to Edwardian England, was paid for by Sir Henry Pellatt, the wealthy Canadian financier who later realized his dream of building the Casa Loma castle. events.title: 3D Printing Demo

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. events.title: Bars & Beats
events.description: Coach Desiree McKenzie guides participants through an exploration of rap music as poetry in motion. Participants will analyze lyrics and create their own original spoken word pieces inspired by musical flow and technique.

events.title: Teen Quiet Study
events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. Tables and chairs are provided, as well as a variety of materials to take study breaks. This is a drop-in program for Teens, and space is limited.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. In this talk, James Rowan explores the current landscape of AI tools in the creative industries, focusing on how artists, designers, and media practitioners are beginning to integrate them into their workfl

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Rulers in Space: Measuring Distances in the Universe
events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? Grab your cosmic ruler and embark on an exploration of the methods used to infer distances in the Universe — the result of centuries of innovative thinking by countless generations of astronomers. Join Dr. Gustavo Medina Toledo, a postdoctoral researcher from Dunlap Institute for Astronomy & Astrophysics at the University of Toronto, for a fascinating talk on this topic. Registration is en

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time

events.title: Pride Planet Cross Stitch
events.description: Join us at the Merril Collection for an introductory cross stitch workshop. Participants will stitch a Pride flag inspired ringed planet. Enjoy our current exhibit, Solar System Stories , and learn a new skill. This workshop is designed for beginners with no sewing or cross stitch experience. All supplies will be provided.
